mission.title: Austria rehab facilities — local dynamic census
mission.original_prompt: Full census of rehabilitation, addiction treatment, and mental-health residential facilities across Austria. Prefer official directories, hospital sites, clinic websites, and regional coverage.

mission_summary.goal: To produce a comprehensive, regionally granular census of all residential rehabilitation, addiction treatment, and mental-health facilities in Austria, leveraging official directories and authoritative sources.
target_entities: Addiction treatment facilities (drug, alcohol, gambling, etc.)
Mental-health residential facilities (psychiatric clinics, long-term care)
Hospitals with dedicated rehab/addiction/mental-health residential units
deliverables: Structured dataset (JSON or CSV) of all identified Austrian rehabilitation, addiction treatment, and mental-health residential facilities, with required fields per schema.
Documentation of data provenance (source links, extraction dates).
Coverage notes describing gaps, uncertainties, and regional completeness.

included: Rehabilitation, addiction treatment, and mental-health residential facilities with inpatient or residential capacity in Austria, both public and private, for adults and youth.

source_strategy.source_type: Federal and provincial government health directories (e.g., gesundheit.gv.at, Landesgesundheitsfonds websites)
Austrian Social Insurance provider directories (e.g., sozialversicherung.at)
source_strategy.purpose: Supplement official lists with insurance network data for coverage of contracted facilities

source_strategy.source_type: Professional association and major provider directories (e.g., privatkrankenanstalten.at, suchthilfe.wien, VAMED)
source_strategy.purpose: Fill gaps and cross-check using professional associations and major provider group listings

source_strategy.source_type: Commercial health provider directories (e.g., docfinder.at, netdoktor.at, herold.at)
source_strategy.purpose: Discover additional private/NGO facilities and cross-validate entries
Enrich and verify specific facility details as needed

data_schema.description: The official or commonly used name of the facility.
Full address including street, city, postal code, and Bundesland (state).
Bundesland (federal state) where the facility is located.
Facility type(s): rehabilitation, addiction treatment, mental health residential.
Ownership/Operator: public, private, NGO, religious, or mixed.
List of specializations or services (e.g., alcohol, opioids, dual diagnosis, PTSD).
Number of residential beds or capacity (if published).
Accreditation or licensing body, if specified.
Source or provenance URL(s) for verification.

classification_rules: Classify each facility as one or more of: rehabilitation, addiction treatment, and/or mental health residential.
Use Austrian official terminology (e.g., 'Rehabilitationszentrum', 'Suchtbehandlung', 'Psychiatrie') for facility type.
Allow multi-category assignment if a facility serves several functions (e.g., psychosomatic rehab clinic).
Classify operator/ownership as public, private, NGO, or religious.
Distinguish between inpatient/residential and outpatient-only facilities; only include those with residential/inpatient capacity.
Map specializations (e.g., substance, alcohol, dual diagnosis) using Austrian ICD-10 or equivalent local codes where available.
Document facilities serving adults, youth, or both.
verification_rules: Cross-check each facility against at least one official government or insurance provider directory.
If a facility is found only in non-official sources, attempt to verify via its own website or by cross-referencing with major provider directories.
For ambiguous or conflicting records (e.g., multiple addresses or names), flag for manual or sample-based verification.
Document the exact source and extraction date for each facility record.
Prioritize structured or downloadable data (CSV, Excel, structured HTML) for accuracy; avoid unstructured or unverifiable entries.
For high-uncertainty (private/rural) facilities, supplement with manual checks or note as unverified.
deduplication_rules: Deduplicate by matching facility name + exact address + phone (fuzzy matching for typographical variations).
Where a facility has multiple branches or locations, treat each as a separate entity but group under the parent operator.
Account for dialectal and spelling variants in Austrian German for both region and facility type.
If multiple sources list the same facility with minor differences, cross-verify and merge details, flagging conflicts for review.
compliance_rules: Collect only institutional, non-personal data (no patient or staff names, no individualized contact details).
Prioritize scraping from official, public, machine-readable directories and avoid circumvention of access controls.
Do not process or store data outside the EEA without proper safeguards.
If a website or source requires login, payment, or explicit consent to access data, do not scrape it.
Cease scraping if robots.txt or explicit site terms prohibit automated collection.
Aggregate only public, institutional information; do not infer or publish potentially identifying dynamic operational data.
Respect DSGVO/GDPR and Austrian DSG for all data processing; if uncertainty exists, flag for DPIA (data protection impact assessment).
Use German-language facility and service terms, adapting for regional naming and dialects as needed.
If data is only available via direct inquiry or FOIA request, flag for manual follow-up rather than scraping.

task_plan.task: Compile a list of all relevant federal and provincial health directories and public insurance provider directories.
Define full set of required data fields and map Austrian terms for facility types and regions.
Design data schema, deduplication, and verification rules; map specializations using Austrian codes where possible.
Identify state-level terminology, dialectal differences, and coverage expectations for rural and urban regions.
Validate all source and field-level compliance with DSGVO/GDPR and Austrian law; flag any DPIA or legal ambiguities.
Scrape and aggregate facility data from all Tier 1 sources, followed by Tier 2 and 3 sources for gap-filling.
Deduplicate, cross-reference, and validate facility records; flag entries needing manual review.
Review output for regional completeness and note any structural or regional data gaps.
Assemble final dataset with provenance, metadata, and coverage notes.

stop_conditions: Source requires login, payment, or explicit per-request consent.
Robots.txt or terms of service prohibit automated access or scraping.
Source data contains personal or identifying information about individuals (patients or staff).
Website or database is behind a paywall or authentication barrier.
Discovery that harvesting would result in profiling or indirect identification of individuals.
Requirement to process data outside EEA without adequate protections.
Any legal notice or policy indicating scraping is not permitted.

notes: Full coverage requires targeting all 9 Austrian Bundesländer individually due to decentralized health administration.
Official directories may cover approximately 80% of facilities; private/NGO clinics could require manual follow-up.
Estimated 500–1,000 total facilities based on population and known health sector size.
20–30% of facilities (especially private or rural) likely require manual validation or enrichment.
Workload may increase if official directories are not machine-readable (e.g., presented as PDFs or non-structured HTML).
Unknowns include the presence of anti-scraping measures, frequency of source updates, and completeness of public listings.

agent_assignments.responsibility: Provide country and cultural context; identify terminology and regional differences.
Design and prioritize the source discovery and search strategy.
Assess legal, regulatory, and compliance environment.
Define data schema, classification, verification, and deduplication rules.

runs.planner_rationale: A team of five is optimal given the national scope across all Austrian regions, the mix of official and non-official sources, the compliance requirements under DSGVO/GDPR, the need for German-language and regional expertise, and the complexity of deduplication and quality control. Each agent has distinct responsibilities: mission interpretation and schema translation, Austrian regional and terminology expertise, source strategy and tiering, regulatory compliance, and an independent agent for deduplication, verification, and quality control. This structure ensures comprehensive coverage and minimizes data and compliance risks.

plan_json.rationale: A team of five is optimal given the national scope across all Austrian regions, the mix of official and non-official sources, the compliance requirements under DSGVO/GDPR, the need for German-language and regional expertise, and the complexity of deduplication and quality control. Each agent has distinct responsibilities: mission interpretation and schema translation, Austrian regional and terminology expertise, source strategy and tiering, regulatory compliance, and an independent agent for deduplication, verification, and quality control. This structure ensures comprehensive coverage and minimizes data and compliance risks.

agents.name: Mission Interpreter
agents.purpose: Interpret the mission, finalize requirements, and ensure the blueprint is fully operationalized.
agents.instructions: Translate blueprint into actionable scraping requirements, define field mapping, and synchronize terminologies with Austrian health context.

assigned_scope.focus: Facility type mapping, deliverables, schema translation

agents.name: Austrian Regional & Terminology Researcher
agents.purpose: Provide Austrian country, Bundesland, and dialect context; ensure terminology and regional completeness.
agents.instructions: Map regional differences, dialectal terms, and expected facility categories for each Austrian Bundesland; flag rural/urban data gaps.

agents.name: Source Strategy & Aggregation Expert
agents.purpose: Design and sequence the source discovery and aggregation strategy across all trust tiers.
agents.instructions: Compile, prioritize, and plan extraction approach for Tier 1–4 sources; ensure regional and sectoral coverage; avoid restricted sources.

agents.name: Regulation & Compliance Analyst
agents.purpose: Assess data protection, legal, and ethical compliance for all planned scraping activities.
agents.instructions: Review source access policies, GDPR/DSGVO/DSG compliance, and flag any legal or ethical ambiguities; determine stop conditions.

assigned_scope.focus: DSGVO, Austrian DSG, robots.txt, institutional data only

agents.name: Deduplication & Quality Control Judge
agents.purpose: Verify, deduplicate, and ensure the accuracy and consistency of facility records.
agents.instructions: Apply deduplication rules, cross-validate entries, flag ambiguous records, and review regional completeness; provide final quality check.

assigned_scope.focus: Deduplication, cross-verification, regional completeness, record flagging

source_categories: Federal and provincial government health directories (e.g., gesundheit.gv.at, Landesgesundheitsfonds websites)
